title: Pyright-Zero Cleanup (latent type-defect sweep) — reserved-gap (HITL 2026-06-04)
phase: MERGED — pyright 0/0; all six items A–F resolved; merged to local main
ac_ids: none — reserved-gap cleanup; no AC (acceptance.md locked at AC-073)

commits.subject: fix(types): require kind on UnknownEmbedderEvent (clears A+B)
test: targeted pyright ignores for test-only hooks + optional deps; py3.10 tomllib fallback (C/E/F)
fix(test): relocate pyright negative-error fixture outside include + repoint test (item D)

findings_addressed.mechanism: Dropped `total=False` from `class UnknownEmbedderEvent(TypedDict)` so `kind` is REQUIRED (total TypedDict). Every native-emitted event carries a `kind` discriminant, so `event["kind"]` on the bare union is sound. Runtime shape unchanged (still a plain dict; key was always present; no Required[] import needed). Added a docstring line stating kind is required. Keystone fix; also cleared B.
AUTO-CLEARED by A (no separate edit, no ignore added — exactly as §2 anticipated). Verified: pyright reports 0 errors at :61/:74 after the types.py totality fix, with those lines untouched.

findings_addressed.class: Test-only FFI hooks (absent on the release binding)
findings_addressed.mechanism: Extended the existing :132-style targeted suppression: line-scoped `# type: ignore[attr-defined]` on the test-hooks-gated native seam calls, matching the file's existing style. These methods are deliberately absent from the release binding's `_fathomdb.pyi` stub.

findings_addressed.class: Intentional-error fixture, analyzed only by the subprocess test
findings_addressed.mechanism: CORRECTED mechanism (item D's enumerated fallback: fixture-outside-include + repoint-test). The original prompt premise was FALSE vs on-disk state: test_pyright_flags_unnarrowed_variant_key_access (lines 121-146) asserts errors_on_unsafe is NON-EMPTY, whose ONLY source is fixture line 30's `_unsafe = event["bytes"]` — so a `# pyright: ignore` there suppresses the error and BREAKS that test; and `[tool.pyright] exclude` drops explicitly-passed paths (prior implementer proved 0 diagnostics, breaks both subprocess tests). Fix: `git mv` the fixture to a NEW dir src/python/_typecheck_fixtures/ (directly under src/python, OUTSIDE include=["fathomdb","tests"] and outside fathomdb/). agent-verify typecheck runs `pyright -p src/python` with NO file args -> analyzes only `include` -> the fixture leaves the project lint surface (project pyright now 0/0). The subprocess test passes the fixture path EXPLICITLY with `--project pyproject.toml`; pyright analyzes explicitly-passed files regardless of `include` (file is not in `exclude`) -> both the reveal_type narrowing AND the line-30 bytes-error assertion still fire. Fixture content byte-for-byte unchanged (100% rename; line 30 and its `# pyright: error` comment untouched). Only the test's _FIXTURE path constant repointed: `Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "_typecheck_fixtures" / "_pyright_narrowing_fixture.py"`. No behavioral assertion touched (_EXPECTED_REVEALS and the errors_on_unsafe assertion both intact).
findings_addressed.proof: GATE1 `cd src/python && python -m pyright` = 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 informations. GATE2 `pytest src/python/tests/test_pyright_narrowing.py -v` = 2 passed (test_pyright_narrows_embedder_event_variants AND test_pyright_flags_unnarrowed_variant_key_access — the bytes-error assertion still fires from the explicitly-passed relocated fixture). Fixture NOT collected by pytest (leading underscore + outside tests/; `pytest src/python/_typecheck_fixtures --collect-only` = 0 items). Not packaged by maturin build (python-source='.', module-name fathomdb._fathomdb; the wheel packages the `fathomdb` module — _typecheck_fixtures is a sibling top-level dir, not under fathomdb/).

findings_addressed.mechanism: Targeted `# pyright: ignore[reportMissingImports]` on the already-try/except-guarded import so the result is env-independent (0 whether or not the optional [dev]/[test] dep is installed). In this env [dev] installs hypothesis so E did not appear in the RED 11, but the ignore is kept for CI/agent-verify runs without the extra. Did NOT install the dep as the fix.

findings_addressed.class: Env-only AND latent real bug (tomllib stdlib only on 3.11+, repo targets >=3.10)
findings_addressed.mechanism: Replaced bare `import tomllib` with a version-guarded fallback (`import sys` added): `if sys.version_info >= (3, 11): import tomllib` / `else:  # pragma: no cover` / `    import tomli as tomllib  # pyright: ignore[reportMissingImports]`. tomllib is stdlib only on 3.11+ but the repo targets >=3.10 and pyright is pinned to 3.10, so the bare import would raise ModuleNotFoundError at collection on 3.10. tomli is not a declared dep, so the else-branch targeted ignore is honest (branch never taken on 3.11+ runtime; type surface identical across branches).

tdd_evidence.red_count_note: Original RED baseline (full inventory) = 11 errors. After A/B/C/E/F landed (af3d57b), residual RED was exactly 5 errors, ALL from item D's fixture: 3x reportGeneralTypeIssues (line 30 `event["bytes"]` not a key in DefaultEmbedderCacheHitEvent / MeanVecPinnedEvent / UnknownEmbedderEvent) + 2x malformed-directive (`# pyright: error` not on its own line; `error` unknown directive). After D relocation: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 informations.
red_errors: tests/_pyright_narrowing_fixture.py:30:37 'pyright comments must appear on their own line' (D, malformed-directive)
tests/_pyright_narrowing_fixture.py:30:46 '"error" is an unknown directive' (D, malformed-directive)
tdd_evidence.note_on_E: E (hypothesis reportMissingImports) was NOT in the RED 11 because this worktree env has [dev] installed (hypothesis resolves). The targeted ignore is added anyway for env-independence per §3.0.
tdd_evidence.pyright_after_ACEF: 5 errors, 0 warnings — entire residual was item D's fixture cluster.
test_files: src/python/tests/test_pyright_narrowing.py (D repointed _FIXTURE constant only; both functions pass, 2/2)

blockers_encountered.summary: Prior implementer correctly STOPPED at item D: the prompt's item-D premise (the subprocess test 'does NOT assert the line-30 negative error') was FALSE against on-disk state. test_pyright_flags_unnarrowed_variant_key_access (lines 121-146) asserts errors_on_unsafe is non-empty, whose sole source is fixture line 30. Both prompt-prescribed mechanisms (targeted ignore on line 30; `[tool.pyright] exclude` the fixture) break a green: the ignore suppresses the asserted error; exclude drops explicitly-passed paths (0 diagnostics).
blockers_encountered.resolution: Orchestrator verified the blocker against on-disk state and amended item D to its own enumerated fallback (the prior implementer's recommended Option 1): relocate the fixture to a NEW dir src/python/_typecheck_fixtures/ OUTSIDE include, and repoint the test's _FIXTURE path constant. This decouples the project-wide run (which no longer analyzes the fixture -> 0/0) from the subprocess run (which passes the path explicitly -> still sees reveals AND the bytes error). Both gates verified green. The original prompt-D premise being false vs on-disk test is recorded as the root cause of the escalation.
blockers_encountered.retry_note: Prior implementer respected the §7 retry budget (proved both mechanisms fail, STOPPED, did not weaken a guardrail). This resumed run implemented only the amended mechanism.

additional_changes_made_in_scope.D_mechanism_chosen: relocate-outside-include + repoint-test (item D's enumerated fallback). Original prompt-D premise (subprocess test does not assert the line-30 negative) was false vs the on-disk test; recorded.
additional_changes_made_in_scope.tomllib_fallback_shape: import sys; `if sys.version_info >= (3, 11): import tomllib` else `# pragma: no cover` -> `import tomli as tomllib  # pyright: ignore[reportMissingImports]`

Project pyright independently 0/0.

The only `total=False` prose hits were this slice's own prompt + historical run-logs/STATUS-0.8.0.md (not contract docs) — left untouched per 'do not manufacture docs'. mkdocs NOT run (no docs/ or nav touched).

No divergence introduced (A is additive-safe; a kind was always emitted).
